title: Trying DeepSeek Flash for Stock Research: Why I Built an Ontology-Driven Retriever
author: Big-Television6736
selftext:
Hello, I’m a retail investor living in Korea.

When I did stock research with AI, I kept hitting two pain points:

the cost was too high, and I always felt the results were just a little bit off—around 2%.

So I decided to try the cheapest practical option, DeepSeek Flash.

The important part wasn’t just the model swap.

I structured things so DeepSeek searches through a dedicated company information DB first (for now, I call it an ontology) and then generates answers from that.

The results were better than I expected.

Even people who aren’t very into stock investing can benefit from this, because they can follow the evidence path and validate the conclusion.

My next goal is to reach similar performance with a local LLM.

If that happens, this would be much more cost-effective for everyday users like me.

It is currently free since this is still in the experimental stage. Thank you.

https://preview.redd.it/kcjc3f8rqh6h1.png?width=1756&format=png&auto=webp&s=b98b506fc07a18f78496a9a654b74ec71570e2ee

https://preview.redd.it/lbrc6g8rqh6h1.png?width=1762&format=png&auto=webp&s=2d64b85012af3ff9eb2ee86d8fadc335f11ceb80

comments.author: MycoHost01
comments.body:
Am using DeepSeek to trade sol perpetuals on a sol decentralized perpetual program I was able to index all events from the program that it emits for data to feed to DeepSeek! So it can wake on a cycle loook at the data and make a decision enter short enter long or hold and if it’s holding a position it only has two decisions to make hold or close position. It may sound familiar I’m going with the same approach as nof1 but with way better data for the llm 

So it’s actively being fed current live data so that it can make trade decisions Iv been having great results over the last few weeks compared to when it first went live! For the price it’s amazing I only spend at most 25 cents a day and that’s with three agents running all day
